profile.name: 童家豪 (Tong Jiahao)
profile.availability: Open to senior frontend and product engineering roles
profile.summary: I build calm, fast, and maintainable digital products with a strong sense for product detail, information architecture, and craft.
bio: 过去 7 年专注于 Web 产品、设计系统与数据可视化，习惯从业务目标、用户任务和工程可维护性之间寻找平衡点。
我偏好小而稳定的团队，喜欢把复杂流程整理成可复用的系统，并用清晰的界面帮助用户更快做出判断。

experience.description: Led frontend architecture for a multi-tenant analytics product used by growth, finance, and operations teams.
highlights: Built a composable dashboard framework that reduced new report delivery time by 45%.
Introduced shared interaction patterns across tables, charts, filters, and export flows.
Partnered with design to establish a white-label component system for enterprise customers.

experience.description: Delivered workflow tools for operations teams, focusing on task triage, permission models, and audit-friendly UI.
highlights: Improved core workflow completion rate by simplifying the review surface.
Migrated legacy pages into a modern React and API-driven architecture.

experience.description: Built campaign sites, internal tools, and content systems for education and culture clients.
highlights: Developed reusable page templates for editorial teams.
Created performance budgets and deployment checklists for small teams.

projects.title: Aurora Dashboard
projects.summary: A modular operating dashboard for revenue, retention, and product usage teams.
projects.description: Aurora Dashboard brings scattered metrics into a flexible work surface. It combines fast filtering, clean chart primitives, and narrative annotations so teams can make decisions without exporting data to separate tools.
projects.role: Frontend architecture, data model, interaction design

projects.title: Folio OS
projects.summary: A calm knowledge workspace for collecting notes, decisions, and reusable research fragments.
projects.description: Folio OS treats notes as living material instead of static pages. It supports connected writing, source tracking, and lightweight review rituals for people who need to turn research into decisions.
projects.role: Product design, frontend implementation, content model

projects.title: Signal Care
projects.summary: A care coordination interface that helps teams prioritize urgent patient follow-ups.
projects.description: Signal Care organizes high-volume follow-up work into a readable command center. The design emphasizes confidence, traceability, and gentle alerting rather than noisy urgency.

articles.title: Designing a Resume as a Living System
articles.excerpt: A resume can be more than a static document when its structure reflects the way you actually work.
content: A good resume site should answer two questions quickly: what kind of work this person does, and whether their judgment feels trustworthy.
The fastest path is not always more visual noise. Clear hierarchy, strong verbs, and carefully selected proof points often communicate seniority better than a crowded page.
Treat the data model as the core product. Once profile, projects, roles, writing, and links are structured, the interface can evolve without rewriting the story each time.

articles.title: White Space as Interface Material
articles.excerpt: White space is not emptiness. It is a way to make decisions feel quieter and faster.
content: White interfaces succeed when spacing, typography, and edges do real work. Without that discipline, a light theme quickly turns into a blank sheet.
The trick is to give every layer a job: background for rest, borders for grouping, subtle color for orientation, and type for momentum.
When the system is restrained, small details become visible. That is where perceived quality often lives.

articles.title: Make Portfolio Projects Prove Something
articles.excerpt: A portfolio project is strongest when it explains the problem, your role, and the measurable change.
content: Screenshots alone rarely tell the whole story. The reader needs to understand the context that made the work difficult.
Strong project pages separate responsibility from outcome. What did you own, what constraints mattered, and what changed after the work shipped?
The most credible portfolios are selective. They use fewer projects, sharper framing, and richer evidence.
